Title: A rearranged Amaranthus palmeri extrachromosomal circular DNA confers resistance to glyphosate and glufosinate

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen assimilation; target process associated with glufosinate herbicide action
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Amplification of GS2.1 is associated with glufosinate resistance; in MSR2 it co-amplifies on rearranged eccDNA with GS2.2 and EPSPS, while in MSR1 GS2.1 alone is amplified with a distinct pattern.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PacBio HiFi eccDNA assembly; long-read coverage; PCR of rearrangement junctions; qPCR CNV assay; dPCR/RT-qPCR copy-number analysis across accessions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported increased transcription in glufosinate-resistant MSR2; detailed tissue pattern NA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Selection under glufosinate ammonium and glyphosate herbicide pressure
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome PacBio HiFi sequencing, BioProject PRJNA1151833; SRA SRR30359587, SRR30359588, SRR30359586, SRR30359583, SRR30359584, SRR30359585
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily relevant as a resistance-monitoring marker rather than crop nutrient engineering target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS2.1 is one of two tandem chloroplastic GS2 genes inserted into a rearranged eccDNA in MSR2. 2. Its copy number increases specifically in glufosinate-resistant plants carrying the rearranged replicon. 3. MSR1 shows GS2.1-only amplification, indicating structurally distinct resistance replicons/mechanisms.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on herbicide resistance rather than nutritional metabolite enhancement; glutamine metabolism is relevant only through target-site amplification.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen assimilation; target process associated with glufosinate herbicide action
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Amplification of GS2.2 is associated with glufosinate resistance in MSR2; GS2.2 is more abundant than GS2.1 and may also occur on an additional GS2.2-only eccDNA variant.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PacBio HiFi eccDNA assembly; long-read coverage; PCR of rearrangement junctions; qPCR CNV assay; isoform-specific RT-qPCR/dPCR correlation analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Selection under glufosinate ammonium and glyphosate herbicide pressure
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome PacBio HiFi sequencing, BioProject PRJNA1151833; SRA SRR30359587, SRR30359588, SRR30359586, SRR30359583, SRR30359584, SRR30359585
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily relevant as a resistance-monitoring marker rather than crop nutrient engineering target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS2.2 is inserted with GS2.1 in the rearranged eccDNA of MSR2. 2. GS2.2 copy number is elevated only in plants carrying the EPSPS+GS2 replicon. 3. GS2.2 abundance exceeds GS2.1, supporting an additional GS2.2-only eccDNA variant in MSR2.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not amplified in MSR1 according to the reported assay.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shikimate-pathway products required for primary metabolism; target process associated with glyphosate herbicide action
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Amplification of EPSPS on eccDNA confers glyphosate resistance and co-occurs with GS2-containing rearranged eccDNA in MSR2, producing dual glyphosate/glufosinate resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PacBio HiFi eccDNA assembly; long-read coverage at native locus and eccDNA; PCR markers; qPCR CNV; dPCR correlation analyses
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Selection under glyphosate and glufosinate ammonium herbicide pressure
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome PacBio HiFi sequencing, BioProject PRJNA1151833; SRA SRR30359587, SRR30359588, SRR30359586, SRR30359583, SRR30359584, SRR30359585; previous eccDNA accession MT025716; new eccDNA accession PQ252370
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a molecular marker for herbicide-resistance surveillance; not a nutrient-quality target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPSPS remains present in the original glyphosate-resistance eccDNA and in the rearranged EPSPS+GS2 eccDNA. 2. Higher EPSPS copy number correlates with resistance and reflects coexistence of multiple eccDNA variants. 3. In MSR2, EPSPS coduplication with GS2 underlies dual resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study is centered on adaptive herbicide resistance rather than beneficial nutrient accumulation.
